<!-- Generated by full-crm-platform-discovery workflow (36 agents, 26 domains) on 2026-06-27. Honest maturity: 13%. Companion: DATA-OPS-AUDIT.md, CRM-PARITY-HUBSPOT-ZOHO.md, BULK-OPS-AUDIT.md. -->

> ⚠️ **CORRECTED 2026-06-28 — read `CRM-CAPABILITY-CENSUS.md` alongside this.** A capability census (live HubSpot MCP + competitor inventory + code-grounded audit) found this doc (a) UNDER-RATED import/data-ops (rated MEDIUM-HIGH/Phase 3 → actually CRITICAL/near-term for a bulk-migrated CRM), (b) WRONGLY listed record-merge as MISSING (it exists, ALT-293, but is non-atomic — a live risk, ALT-416), and (c) OMITTED ~12 table-stakes items (import history/rollback, skipped-row file, mapping templates, validation rules, dependent picklists, record types, tags, suppression lists, blueprint/approval engine, background job queue — ALT-416..427). Where the two docs disagree, the census is newer.

> **✅/🟡 status tags added 2026-06-28 — items marked ✅ IMPLEMENTED are DONE (don't re-build); 🟡 PARTIAL = partly done; untagged = still open. Verify against code before acting.**

## Top takeaways (the punchline)

1. It is a UI over a frozen DB, not a platform. The three things that make software a CRM platform rather than a database-with-a-skin — a configurable data model, an automation/event engine, and an API/integration surface — are each essentially absent. Every new field is a migration; every new behavior is code. Until features are added as DATA (metadata registry) instead of as code, daily shipping stays slow and fragile.

2. Intelligence is at literal 0% and is blocked UPSTREAM by capture, not by AI. There are no LLM calls, no pgvector, no embeddings, no scoring, no sentiment, no intent anywhere. But the real blocker is that we capture almost no conversations: no email capture, no call recording/transcription, no meeting transcripts. You cannot derive intent from data you never captured. Capture (email + calls + meetings into one spine) must precede any intelligence work.

3. The append-only interaction table is the single most valuable existing asset — it is the embryo of the event/activity SPINE. Generalize it into one immutable `event` table where every conversation (any channel, any source) lands as a row with a clean `body_text` projection. That one column is the contract that decouples capture from intelligence: capturers write body_text, the AI layer reads body_text, and the two evolve independently.

4. Identity resolution is the unowned keystone of 'ONE place'. Nothing in the system decides that a call, an email thread, a LinkedIn DM, and a meeting attendee are the SAME person/company across channels. Without an entity-resolution layer, 'capture everything in one place' is impossible no matter how many connectors are built — the data stays siloed by channel.

5. The mini-goal's anti-fragility requirement ('one change cannot silently crash dependents') is barely addressed and must be built FIRST, not last. It needs concrete mechanisms: typed contracts (zod/OpenAPI) at every web/notify-service/DB seam, feature flags + kill switches on every layer, a transactional outbox for durable/retryable actions, append-only events so writers can only add, versioned APIs so schema changes hide behind a stable contract, and CI/contract tests + observability. These are engineering disciplines applied AS each layer is built, or each new layer compounds the fragility.

6. Permissions/RLS is genuinely our one mature, near-CRM-grade domain (~70%) — but it is also where the dangerous, quiet security debt lives: no read/access audit trail (attribution is write-only), cross-project master-data leakage (company/contact rows are globally visible while leads are project-scoped), per-IP-only rate limiting, and notify-service endpoints lacking recipient/privilege-escalation validation. Plus, access is coupled to business logic (created_by/RLS), so a permission change can crash a query path — it needs decoupling.

7. We are NOT trying to beat HubSpot or ZoomInfo; we want BOTH in one, and that is reachable by ADDITION, never a rewrite — IF the order is right. Spine -> capture connectors -> metadata registry -> outbox (automation + webhooks) -> API -> intelligence -> MCP. Each layer is additive and reversible (dual-write + backfill + flag-flip, never cut-over-in-place). Get the order wrong and you build intelligence on no data, or build fields-as-code forever.

8. For the actual launch (daily internal use by a calling/outreach team that UPDATES assigned records), we are closer to viable than 13% suggests — RLS, masking, leads, the interaction spine, tasks, reminders, and the Chrome extension already cover the 'update what's assigned to me' loop. The smallest safe slice is: event spine + one capture path (call_log) + metadata-driven fields + ONE depth-capped automation + a versioned read-API seam. Compliance (DPDP/GDPR call-recording consent, retention, PII-at-rest) must ride alongside capture, not after — recording every conversation is a legal landmine.

---

# AltLeads CRM — Product Discovery, Competitive Capability Map & Architecture to the North Star

> **North star:** ONE intelligent system that captures EVERY conversation (calls, emails, meetings, notes, intent signals) in ONE place and drives INTELLIGENCE + INTENT out of it. We are not trying to beat HubSpot/Zoho OR ZoomInfo/Apollo — we want BOTH in one: a real CRM SYSTEM **plus** native intelligence built from our own captured data.
>
> **Mini-goal:** launch internally for the team, usable DAILY, shippable in daily increments, ENGINEERED so one change cannot silently crash dependents.
>
> **Honest baseline being measured:** a polished UI over a single frozen `company / contact / lead / meeting / interaction / task` schema. No object/field/module editor, no automation, no sequences, no email engine, no API/webhook/MCP layer, no native intelligence.

---

## Part A — Honest maturity read (unsparing)

**Where we actually are: ~13% of a real CRM platform; the intelligence half is at 0%.**

Counting the 26 capability domains by depth:

- **1 domain near-mature** — Permissions / RLS / masking (~70%): genuinely well-engineered, defense-in-depth, the one CRM-grade area.
- **3–4 domains with real partial foundations (~30–40%)** — the `interaction` activity spine, Tasks (types/priority/reminders/soft-delete), data-hygiene primitives (soft-delete + audit columns + exact-match dedup), and per-user views/Kanban group-by.
- **2 domains with a thin-but-real seam** — the Chrome extension (in-context LinkedIn capture) and the STAGED `call_log` table with `recording_url`/`transcript` columns reserved (unapplied).
- **~20 domains at 0–10%** — objects/fields/modules, automation, sequences, email engine, telephony, scheduling automation, segmentation, reporting, API, webhooks, MCP, integrations, and ALL native intelligence.

**Why so low despite a polished UI.** Structurally the product is a read/write skin over a frozen schema. The three things that make software *a CRM platform* rather than *a database with a UI* — (1) a configurable data model, (2) an automation/event engine, (3) an integration/API surface — are each essentially absent. Every new field is a migration plus a code change. And the thing that makes it *our* north star — intelligence from captured conversations — is at literal zero, blocked upstream by the fact that we capture almost no conversations. We log dispositions and free-text notes; we do **not** capture the conversation itself (no email capture, no call recording/transcription, no meeting transcripts).

**The fair counter-framing.** For *daily internal use by a calling/outreach team that UPDATES assigned records*, we are closer to viable than 13% suggests — RLS, masking, leads, activities, tasks, reminders, and the extension already cover the "update what's assigned to me" loop. But measured against "a real CRM platform PLUS native intelligence," 13% is the honest number and the intelligence half is 0%.

---

## Part B — Capability map by domain (HAVE / PARTIAL / MISSING + gap severity)

### 1. Objects, Schema & Field System — **CRITICAL**
- HAVE: 7 fixed entities with explicit TS interfaces (`companies.ts`, `contacts.ts`, `realLeads.ts`); hardcoded enums; admin-configurable lookup values via `dropdown_option`; pre-sales free-text Q&A; reference-data editor.
- PARTIAL: dropdown options are flat key/value (no dependent/conditional picklists); pre-sales answers are free-text only.
- MISSING: custom objects, custom field creation, a field-type system, field-level permissions, record types/variants, calculation/rollup/formula fields, property sync, conditional field logic, dynamic schema. **Every schema change is a migration.** The single largest structural gap.

### 2. Field / Property Editor — **CRITICAL**
- HAVE: per-user column visibility/reorder (`ColumnCustomizer`, `user_view_pref`); contact detail-column masking via `contact_master_masked` VIEW + base-table REVOKE; audit columns on all tables.
- PARTIAL: column reconcile toggles visibility/order only; EditableGrid has per-column `disabledReason` but no admin UI.
- MISSING: field-editor UI, validation engine, conditional visibility/dependencies, picklist management UI, formula fields, per-field history, broad PII classification, auto-fill, relationship-field management, default values, type conversion.

### 3. Module & Page-Layout Builder — **HIGH**
- HAVE: ViewSwitcher (Table/Grid/Kanban) persisted per user; EditableGrid inline editing; read-only GenericKanban with group-by; per-project status; 8-tab admin panel.
- PARTIAL: Kanban drag-to-change-stage seam exists but is NOT wired (read-only, ALT-292); stages are free-form strings.
- MISSING: page-layout builder, dynamic forms/visibility rules, configurable related lists, custom buttons/actions, compact/record-type layouts, card customization, embedded components, pipeline/stage management UI.

### 4. Deals, Pipelines & Forecasting — **CRITICAL** (for a sales CRM)
- HAVE: Lead object (`lead_master` + `lead_report`) with a `stage` string; hardcoded approval workflow; per-project working status; activity timeline.
- PARTIAL: stages are uncontrolled strings; no probability, no forecast category, no canonical stage table.
- MISSING: a real deal/opportunity object, pipeline/stage config, probability weighting, forecast categories/engine, line items/CPQ/quotes, weighted amounts, win-loss analysis, deal scoring/risk, stagnation detection, forecast dashboards.

### 5. Automation & Workflow Engine — **CRITICAL**
- HAVE: a single background task-reminder scanner (per-task + daily digest, in-app + email) in notify-service; approval workflow hardcoded to fixed stages.
- MISSING: the ENTIRE engine — triggers, branching, conditions, action library, schedules, webhooks, approval-matrix config, Blueprints/state machines, custom-code actions, monitoring. Table-stakes, wholly absent beyond one cron job.

### 6. Sequences & Cadences — **CRITICAL** (for an outreach north-star)
- MISSING entirely. No sequence builder, no cadence model, no enrollment/unenrollment, no multi-channel orchestration, no sequence analytics. The team's core motion (systematic outreach) has zero engine.

### 7. Email Engine (capture / send / automate / intelligence) — **CRITICAL**
- HAVE: transactional email via notify-service (templated events: lead_assigned, meeting_scheduled, task_reminder).
- MISSING: native send-from-CRM, automatic email capture/logging, threading, open/click/reply tracking, bounce handling, templates/composer, personalization, sequences, suppression/compliance, deliverability/warmup, inbox plugins, two-way sync. This is half the north star — email is currently invisible to the CRM; real conversations live in Gmail/Outlook.

### 8. Telephony / Calls — **CRITICAL** (for the north star)
- HAVE: call logging (`logCall` → interaction) with dispositions; call-log preview/history; STAGED (unapplied) `public.call_log` with NULLABLE `recording_url` + `transcript` seams.
- PARTIAL: call_log table staged but not in prod; recording/transcript columns reserved but unused.
- MISSING: click-to-call, dialer, recording, transcription/diarization, disposition automation, IVR/routing, PII redaction, sentiment, QA/coaching, analytics, mobile calling. We log a disposition; the richest conversation signal — the actual call — is entirely uncaptured.

### 9. Meetings / Scheduling — **HIGH**
- HAVE: meeting entity with modes/statuses; meeting creation triggered by approval; post-meeting 7-question feedback stored in `feedback_answer`; meeting-scheduled emails.
- PARTIAL: feedback answers captured but nothing analyzes them.
- MISSING: booking links, calendar sync, round-robin/routing, reminders, no-show analytics, availability rules, video-conf links, transcription/auto-notes, conversation intelligence, pre-meeting briefings.

### 10. Activities Timeline & Tasks — **MEDIUM-HIGH**
- HAVE: append-only `interaction` log (record_type/type/disposition/note_text/occurred_at); org-wide timeline read; task module (types/status/priority/due_at/reminders/soft-deletes); per-record call history.
- PARTIAL: timeline returns raw rows; no cross-channel unification beyond manual logging; no AI insights.
- MISSING: auto-capture from email/calendar/calls, custom activity types, task queues, pinned activities, multi-threaded engagement, @mention collaboration, activity API. **The interaction table is the right shape for an activity spine — but it only holds manually-logged events.**

### 11. Lists & Segmentation — **HIGH**
- HAVE: per-user saved views (column sets); Kanban group-by; per-project status filters. — ✅ IMPLEMENTED (ColumnCustomizer + user_view_pref + DensityToggle + ViewSwitcher; column-layout + density saved views done)
- PARTIAL: saved "views" are layout prefs, not membership segments. — 🟡 PARTIAL (layout views ✅ done; filter SEGMENTS still open ALT-404)
- MISSING: dynamic/static segments, advanced filter logic (AND/OR/nested), suppression, list-triggered automation, association filtering, RFM/scoring, rollups, rolling dates, membership API. A hard blocker for any campaign or targeted outreach.

### 12. Reporting & Analytics — **HIGH**
- HAVE: read-only hardcoded dashboard; Excel export; activity timeline as a raw feed.
- MISSING: report builder, pivots, cross-object reports, dashboard builder, scheduled reports, attribution, predictive forecasting, conversation/intent analytics, audit-log reporting, embedded BI.

### 13. Data Import / Quality — **MEDIUM-HIGH** (was rated MEDIUM-HIGH → actually CRITICAL per census)
- HAVE: soft-deletes everywhere; audit columns; exact-match dedup (`findDuplicates.ts`: email/phone/linkedin_clean/domain_clean); migration CLI for bulk data.
- PARTIAL: dedup is exact-match only; soft-delete exists but no recycle-bin/restore/undo UI.
- MISSING: import wizard / field mapping, fuzzy/semantic dedup, bulk merge UI, validation-on-import, recycle bin/undo, per-field history, enrichment, bulk-action queue with progress/cancel.
  - 🟡 PARTIAL — import wizard: FE wizard shipped (ImportPage.tsx ALT-399: validates + preview + cleaned-file download); server-side save endpoint gated (coming soon). Bulk-action queue with progress/cancel: ✅ IMPLEMENTED (BulkProgressBar.tsx + bulkActions.ts ALT-401). Duplicate detection: ✅ IMPLEMENTED (findDuplicates.ts + DuplicatesButton.tsx ALT-394). Record merge: 🟡 PARTIAL (ALT-293 exists but non-atomic — live risk; atomic staged ALT-416).

### 14. API / Developer Platform — **CRITICAL**
- HAVE: Supabase PostgREST (RLS-enforced client CRUD); notify-service admin endpoints (user create/reset, login-coverage) gated by `requireAdmin`; PG trigger functions (internal); extension RPCs.
- PARTIAL: Realtime connected for auth/session only.
- MISSING: public/developer REST API, API keys, GraphQL, webhooks, OAuth, MCP server, queues, serverless custom-code, sandbox, metadata API, marketplace, outbound integration clients. No public surface, no event system.

### 15. Webhooks & Events — **CRITICAL**
- MISSING entirely. PG triggers fire internal functions only; nothing emits to or ingests from external systems. No event-driven architecture means tight synchronous coupling — directly at odds with the anti-fragility goal.

### 16. AI Copilot, Predictive & Intelligence — **CRITICAL** (the defining half)
- HAVE: a free-text `sales_intelligence` narrative field (manual); the interaction log + dispositions; in-memory keyword global search (`globalSearch.ts`); planning docs (`AI-PGVECTOR-PLAN.md`, `EMBEDDING-PLAN.md` — plan only).
- MISSING (verified zero): no Claude/OpenAI calls anywhere; pgvector NOT enabled; no embeddings table/pipeline/backfill; no semantic search/RAG; no sentiment; no lead/deal scoring; no intent detection; no transcription; no email/meeting transcript capture; no BM25/TF-IDF ranking; no fuzzy/semantic dedup; no manager digests; no similar-accounts; no momentum/health signals. **Downstream of the capture gaps (§7, §8, §10).**

### 17. MCP & Agent Interfaces — **HIGH** (strategic, not daily-blocking)
- MISSING entirely. No MCP server, no tool definitions, no agent framework, no write-safety/dry-run guardrails as a platform primitive (the lead-create skill enforces dry-run per-skill — that pattern should be a platform primitive). Premature until the API/event layer exists.

### 18. Integrations Marketplace — **MEDIUM-HIGH**
- HAVE: Chrome extensions (LinkedIn contact-viewer with masked/unmasked PII; data-research queue with status workflow).
- MISSING: app marketplace, native email/calendar/Slack/WhatsApp, two-way sync, iPaaS, native recording/CI, intent/enrichment, integration framework. The extension is a genuine differentiated asset but stands alone.

### 19. Permissions, Roles, RLS & Sharing — **MEDIUM** (our strongest domain, ~70%)
- HAVE: RLS on all public tables with per-row policies; role-aware predicates (`is_admin()`/`is_qc()`/`current_user_id()`/`manages_project()`); project-scoped lead access with configurable view/edit dials; contact detail-column masking; 6 roles; frontend role guards; admin-only service-role provisioning; Helmet CSP + rate limiting + soft-delete filtering.
- PARTIAL: contact per-project read filtering deferred; company/contact rows globally visible (asymmetric vs project-scoped leads); sales-portal downline RLS pending; project-membership not validated client-side before render.
- MISSING: object/module feature flags; explicit sharing/ACL grants; field-level security beyond contact masking; data-classification labels; **read/access audit logging** (attribution is write-only); per-user (not just per-IP) rate limiting; token revocation; cross-project leakage prevention on master data; per-user permission grants; bulk-export masking.

---

## Part C — Still-missing / under-explored (the seams nobody owns)

The 26 domains above are broad on paper but several keystone capabilities and cross-domain seams are missing:

**North-star capture layer is thin.** No unified conversation data model spanning calls+emails+meetings+notes+WhatsApp/SMS as ONE normalized interaction. **No identity-resolution / entity-resolution engine** — the thing that decides "this call, this email thread, this LinkedIn DM, this meeting attendee are the SAME person/company." That is the keystone of "ONE place" and it is absent. No WhatsApp/SMS capture (the dominant Indian B2B channel). No owned recording-storage + transcription + diarization pipeline. No inbound email full-thread sync (IMAP/Gmail/Graph). No transcript→structured-field extraction loop. No "interaction provenance" (human vs extension vs telephony vs AI) — needed before any intelligence can be trusted.

**Intent & intelligence under-built vs ambition.** No first-party intent scoring from OWN data (reply sentiment, meeting frequency, question/buying-signal phrases) — only third-party intent. No fit-vs-engagement-vs-intent scoring framework. No next-best-action engine. No buying-committee/stakeholder mapping (champion/blocker/economic buyer). No deal-risk/stalled/sentiment-trend detection. No objection/competitor-mention extraction taxonomy. No model-quality/drift monitoring or human-correction capture. **No explainability/citation requirement** — every AI score must link back to its source interaction or the team won't trust it.

**Engineering / anti-fragility (the mini-goal) barely addressed.** No contract/schema-versioning between web↔notify-service↔DB (needs zod/OpenAPI at every seam). No dependency-graph/impact-analysis ("what breaks if I change this field"). No expand-contract migration discipline (additive-first, dual-write, backfill, deprecate). No feature-flag/kill-switch infrastructure. No CI/contract/regression tests. No observability/alerting (Sentry, structured logs, uptime). No RLS-decoupling plan (access is coupled to created_by/RLS). No real job-queue (the cron is ad hoc — automation, sequences, enrichment, transcription all need a worker with retries/idempotency/dead-letter).

**Field-type catalog incomplete.** Missing: formula/rollup, lookup/relationship, multi-select controlled-vocab, currency-with-FX, score/rating, geo/address, file/attachment, rich-text, JSON, encrypted-PII, auto-number, tz-aware datetime. No custom objects. No per-field history. No record-merge. No record-lifecycle state machine.

**CRM table-stakes not surfaced.** Products/line-items/quotes/CPQ; territory/book-of-business rules; lead-routing/round-robin; write-time dedupe + data-quality scoring; goals/quota/forecast-vs-target; multi-currency/timezone/i18n.

**Ecosystem under-specified.** Calendar two-way sync; LinkedIn capture beyond the extension (Sales Nav / message capture); inbound webhook/form-capture/website-visitor tracking; data-warehouse/reverse-ETL/BI export; integration OAuth/secrets/token-refresh framework.

**Governance/compliance thin for a system holding ALL conversations.** No call-recording-consent / DPDP-Act (India) + GDPR handling — recording every conversation is a legal landmine. No retention/purge/right-to-be-forgotten for transcripts. No system-level PII classification + field-level encryption-at-rest (current work is ad-hoc masking). No audit log for AI access to data. No data-residency posture (Supabase region vs Indian localization). No tenant-isolation model for client-portal users.

**Daily-usability/adoption.** No "my day" unified work-queue (calls due + emails + follow-ups + tasks) — adoption depends on ONE screen, not 27 pages. No offline-sync conflict model for field reps. No in-product onboarding / data-trust signals.

**Cross-cutting seams where silent breakage will occur.** Activities-timeline ↔ conversation-intelligence ↔ intent: no single capture→enrich→score→recommend pipeline. Automation ↔ sequences ↔ email-engine ↔ webhooks: four domains that must share ONE event bus; no shared event model defined. Reporting ↔ AI: both need the same metric definitions; no shared metrics/semantic layer, so numbers will diverge.

---

## Part D — Architecture to the north star (engineered for safe daily shipping)

The whole design rests on one bet: **stop adding features as code; start adding them as data.** Two structural pillars must exist before anything else is safe to build daily.

### Pillar A — The Event/Activity Spine (`event` table)
We already have it in embryo: the append-only `interaction` table. Generalize it into ONE immutable `event` table — every conversation, any channel, becomes one event row.
- Append-only (corrections are new events). This is the core anti-fragility primitive: a new writer can only ADD; it can never corrupt what another writer wrote.
- Columns: `event_id`, `event_type` (call|email|meeting|note|status_change|intent_signal|task|automation), `source` (manual|call_tool|email_capture|fathom|extension|automation), `subject_type`+`subject_id`, `actor_user_id`, `occurred_at`, `payload jsonb` (channel body — transcript/headers/disposition), `body_text` (the ONE searchable/embeddable projection), `dedupe_key` (idempotency), audit cols.
- **`body_text` is the contract with the intelligence layer.** Capturers' job ends at "write a clean body_text"; the AI layer only reads body_text + payload. You can add Fathom transcripts Tuesday and the embedder picks them up Wednesday with zero coordination.
- Migration path (non-breaking): `interaction` keeps working; dual-write to `event` via AFTER-INSERT trigger (or `event_compat` VIEW); readers switch behind a flag; backfill once; new channels write only to `event`. Reversible at every step — nothing is destroyed.

### Pillar B — The Metadata Registry (objects & fields become data)
Generalize the existing `dropdown_option` metadata-as-data pattern to objects and fields. Four registry tables (same discipline: soft-delete, audit cols, RLS, admin-only writes):
- `object_def` — logical object, seeded with the 7 existing entities so nothing is special-cased.
- `field_def` — (object_key, field_key, label, data_type∈~12 types, is_required, validation jsonb, options_category, visibility jsonb, storage). One **shared validator** used by BOTH client and write path.
- `field_value` — the EAV escape hatch: custom/new fields land here (value_text/num/date/json) instead of new columns. **New fields never require a migration or deploy.**
- `picklist` — folds in `dropdown_option`.
- Hybrid (native columns + EAV), not pure EAV: existing typed columns stay system-of-record (fast/typed); `field_value` only carries NEW admin-defined fields. A `storage` flag tells the data layer where to read/write; the frontend reads a merged record through one accessor, so pages never know the difference.
- Migration path: zero changes to existing tables — purely additive; first deploy seeds object_def/field_def from current interfaces.

### The five foundation layers (on our stack)
| Layer | Sits on | New surface | Pattern reused |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1. Event Spine | Postgres (`event`), generalize `interaction` | — | append-only, RLS, dual-write trigger |
| 2. Metadata Registry | Postgres (`object_def`/`field_def`/`field_value`) | admin field editor | `dropdown_option` |
| 3. Automation Engine | Postgres outbox + notify-service worker | rule builder | task-reminder scanner |
| 4. API / Webhook | notify-service Express | `/api/v1/*`, webhook dispatch | `requireAdmin`/`requireAuth`, rate limiter |
| 5. Intelligence | pgvector + notify-service AI worker | semantic search, scoring | reads `event.body_text` only |

**Layer 3 — Automation via Transactional Outbox (not in-DB cron).** Generalize the reminder scanner into a rule engine driven by the event stream. `automation_rule` (metadata, admin-editable, every rule ships disabled). Every event INSERT that might trigger automation writes an `outbox` row in the SAME transaction (DB trigger); the worker polls with `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, evaluates rules, performs actions (send_email/create_task/set_field/write_event/call_webhook), retries with backoff. Outbox over Realtime/pg_cron because actions must be retryable + idempotent + dead-lettered. **Cascade containment:** a per-chain `depth` counter + hard ceiling; every action logged as `event(event_type='automation')` so chains are auditable — the explicit guard against "one change silently cascading."

**Layer 4 — API / Webhook (public contract + decoupling).** notify-service becomes the gateway. `/api/v1/` REST, resource-per-object, **metadata-driven** (reads `field_def` to validate/shape — a new field is instantly API-available). Versioned (`v1`) = the change-isolation contract. `api_key` table (hashed, scoped, rate-limited per key — closes the per-IP-only gap). Outbound `webhook_subscription` dispatched by the SAME outbox worker with HMAC + retries + delivery log. Dependents subscribe to events instead of reaching into tables — a schema change behind a stable event contract can't silently break them. MCP deliberately deferred (a thin wrapper over `/api/v1` + the event stream).

**Layer 5 — Intelligence (reads the spine, writes back as events).** Strictly downstream of capture. pgvector enabled; `event_embedding(event_id, embedding)`. An AI worker (same job-runner pattern) consumes new events from the outbox, embeds `body_text`, stores vectors → unblocks semantic search/RAG (replaces in-memory keyword search). Scoring/intent jobs read recent events per record, call the LLM (Anthropic — confirm model/pricing against the `claude-api` skill at build time), and write `intent_signal` events + a derived `record_score` via `field_value` (so scores are just another metadata field, surfaced in lists/Kanban for free). Capture connectors (call_log, Fathom meeting transcripts, email BCC/IMAP, extension) each feed the spine independently behind a flag; intelligence improves automatically as each lights up — no AI code changes.

### Reversibility / blast radius (the safe-daily-shipping core)
- *Reversible & low-blast (additive — ship freely):* Event Spine, Metadata Registry, API skeleton, pgvector tables, every capture connector — they only ADD; the live app doesn't read them until a flag flips.
- *Medium-blast (changes a read path):* repointing `activityTimeline.ts`/`globalSearch.ts` to `event` — mitigate with a compat VIEW + flag (instant rollback).
- *High-blast (could cascade):* EAV read path on hot list pages (behind a per-object flag, one accessor = one-line rollback) and automation actions (ship disabled, dry-run first, depth-capped, every action an event).
- *Never destructive without sign-off:* no DROP/rename of `interaction/company/contact/lead`, no live RLS change without throwaway-role validation. Dual-write + backfill + flip — never cut over in place.

### Three mechanisms that make "one change can't silently crash dependents" structural
1. **Feature flags on every layer** (`feature_flag` table + `useFlag()`): new tables/workers/routes ship dark; flip per-role; rollback = flip a row, no redeploy.
2. **Append-only event spine + transactional outbox:** writers only add; consumers are decoupled + retryable; a broken consumer backs up its own queue, it cannot corrupt producers.
3. **Idempotent, verifying migrations:** the repo's existing applier discipline (embedded SQL, `IF NOT EXISTS`/DO-guards, single BEGIN/COMMIT with ROLLBACK, then VERIFY queries). Every foundation table ships as one such `.cjs` applier — the daily-shipping unit.

---

## Part E — The biggest structural gaps (ranked)

1. **No configurable data model (objects/fields/modules).** Every schema change is a migration. (§1,§2)
2. **No automation/workflow engine.** Beyond one reminder job. (§5)
3. **No email capture or engine.** Conversations live in Gmail; CRM never sees them. (§7)
4. **No call recording/transcription.** Disposition logging only. (§8)
5. **No native intelligence (verified 0%).** No LLM, pgvector, embeddings, scoring, sentiment, intent. (§16)
6. **No API / public surface.** Nothing can integrate or build on us. (§14)
7. **No webhook/event system.** Synchronous, tightly coupled. (§15)
8. **No sequences/cadences.** The outreach team's core motion has no engine. (§6)
9. **No real pipeline/forecasting.** Stages are free-text strings. (§4)
10. **No segmentation engine.** Blocks targeting and campaigns. (§11) — 🟡 PARTIAL (multi-select filters + active-filter chips done; saved filter SEGMENTS still open ALT-404; dynamic/static membership segments not built)
11. **No reporting/dashboard builder.** Fixed dashboard + Excel only. (§12)
12. **No conversation spine / unified timeline + no identity resolution.** The interaction table is the right shape but holds only manual entries; nothing resolves the same person/company across channels. (§10, north-star core)
13. **Schema frozen and brittle by design.** Hardcoded enums/interfaces/migration-only fields ripple through code with no contract/versioning. (cross-cutting)
14. **No access/read-audit trail + cross-project master-data leakage + unvalidated notify endpoints.** Security debt in our strongest area. (§19)
15. **No import/dedup/merge tooling + fuzzy matching + no compliance (DPDP/GDPR consent, retention, PII-at-rest).** (§13, governance) — 🟡 PARTIAL (import wizard FE ✅ ALT-399 gated; dedup detection ✅ ALT-394; bulk-action queue ✅ ALT-401; merge 🟡 ALT-293 non-atomic/ALT-416; fuzzy dedup + compliance still open)

**Strategic read:** gaps #3, #4, #12 (capture) MUST precede #5 (intelligence) — you cannot derive intent from data you never captured. Gaps #6, #7, #2 are the platform backbone. Gaps #13/#14/#15-compliance are the engineering-discipline gaps the mini-goal explicitly calls out — address them AS each layer is built, not after, or each new layer compounds the fragility.

**Net:** the north star is reachable by ADDITION, never a rewrite — if the order is Spine → capture → metadata → outbox(automation+webhooks) → API → intelligence → MCP, each layer additive and reversible, each shipped as one idempotent flag-gated applier.

**Load-bearing files (absolute paths):**
- `c:\Users\pc\OneDrive - Amplior\Desktop\AL\new-code\web\src\data\activityTimeline.ts` — the `interaction` spine to generalize into `event`.
- `c:\Users\pc\OneDrive - Amplior\Desktop\AL\new-code\migration\apply-create-call-log.cjs` — staged `call_log` with `recording_url`/`transcript` seams; the idempotent-applier pattern = the daily-shipping unit.
- `c:\Users\pc\OneDrive - Amplior\Desktop\AL\new-code\web\src\data\dropdowns.ts` — the `dropdown_option` metadata-as-data pattern to generalize into the field registry.
- `c:\Users\pc\OneDrive - Amplior\Desktop\AL\new-code\notify-service\server.js` — `requireAdmin`/`requireAuth` + rate limiter + task-reminder scanner = the API gateway + outbox-worker host.

---

# Phased Roadmap (dependency-aware)

# Phased, Dependency-Aware Roadmap to the North Star

Four phases. Each lists the engineered building blocks **in dependency order**. Every block ships as one idempotent, verifying `.cjs` applier behind a feature flag (additive, reversible via dual-write + backfill + flip — never cut-over-in-place). Hard ordering rules: **Anti-fragility contract before everything → Spine before Intelligence → Metadata Registry before any new field/object work → Outbox before Automation & Webhooks → API before MCP → Capture before Intelligence.**

---

## PHASE 0/1 — FOUNDATION (make adding features safe and data-driven)
*Goal: the structural pillars exist so every later layer is purely additive.*

1. **Anti-fragility baseline** — `feature_flag` table + `useFlag()` hook; kill-switch convention; promote the idempotent-applier pattern as the official daily-shipping unit.
2. **Typed-contract seams** — zod (or OpenAPI) schemas at web↔notify-service↔DB boundaries; one **shared validator** module (used by client AND write path) so a rule change can't disagree across tiers.
3. **Event Spine** — generalize `interaction` into the append-only `event` table (event_type/source/subject/actor/occurred_at/payload/body_text/dedupe_key). Dual-write trigger from `interaction`; `event_compat` VIEW; backfill once; repoint `activityTimeline.ts` + `globalSearch.ts` behind a flag.
4. **Metadata Registry (seed-only)** — `object_def` / `field_def` / `picklist` (fold in `dropdown_option`) seeded from current interfaces; `field_value` EAV table dormant; one merged-record accessor.
5. **Transactional Outbox skeleton** — `outbox` table + AFTER-INSERT trigger on `event`; worker shell in notify-service (FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED, retries/backoff, dead-letter, depth counter). No rules yet.
6. **Observability + CI gate** — structured logs + error tracking (Sentry-class) + uptime; contract/regression tests in CI so daily increments can't silently break a seam.
7. **Job-queue/worker hardening** — replace the ad-hoc cron with the durable outbox worker (idempotency + retries + DLQ).

## PHASE 2 — DAILY-LAUNCH (the team uses it every day to update assigned records)
*Goal: the daily outreach loop — call, log disposition, move stage, note, follow-up — produces events; one safe automation; the API seam exists.*

1. **Capture the daily loop into the spine** — apply the staged `call_log`, dual-write to `event`; wire the Kanban drag-to-stage (ALT-292 seam) to write a `status_change` event.
2. **"My Day" unified work-queue** — ONE screen (calls due + follow-ups + tasks + assigned records) — the adoption surface, not 27 pages.
3. **First custom field end-to-end** — one admin-defined field through `field_def` → `field_value` → merged accessor → list/detail render → API; proves "fields are data, not code."
4. **One automation via outbox (disabled-first)** — "on `event(call, disposition=FOLLOW_UP)` → create follow-up task." Dry-run-logged, depth-capped, every action an event.
5. **Read API v1 + first outbound webhook** — `/api/v1/events` (read, API-key, versioned) + one HMAC-signed webhook on new events; establishes the stable external contract before anyone depends on internals.
6. **Security/access debt fixes (launch-grade)** — read/access audit log; close cross-project master-data (company/contact) leakage; recipient/privilege validation on `/notify` + user-create endpoints; per-user (not just per-IP) rate limiting.
7. **Write-path ownership fix** — agents edit records ASSIGNED to them (not created_by) — the known launch-stopper.
8. **Recycle bin / undo + write-time dedup** — surface existing soft-delete as restore UI; exact-match dedup at write time.

## PHASE 3 — PLATFORM (become a real CRM, not a UI over a DB)
*Goal: configurable model, automation breadth, pipelines, segmentation, email/meeting capture, integrations.*

1. **Field/Property Editor (full)** — ~12 field types, validation engine, conditional visibility + dependent picklists, defaults, per-field history, field-level security.
2. **Page-layout / module builder** — dynamic forms, related lists, custom buttons/actions, record-type layouts, Kanban card config; configurable pipeline/stage objects.
3. **Custom objects** — `object_def`-driven new object types with associations (1:m, m:m junctions).
4. **Deals/Pipelines/Forecasting** — real opportunity object, stage probability, forecast categories, multiple pipelines, win-loss + reason codes; (later) line items/quotes.
5. **Automation Engine (full)** — trigger/condition/action library, branching, delays, schedules, approval-matrix config; all riding the outbox.
6. **Sequences & Cadences** — multi-step multi-channel sequences, auto-enroll/unenroll (reply/bounce), templates, sequence analytics.
7. **Email Engine + capture** — native send/templates/personalization; inbound IMAP/Gmail/Graph two-way thread capture into `event`; tracking/bounce/suppression; deliverability (SPF/DKIM/DMARC).
8. **Meeting capture + scheduling** — calendar two-way sync, booking links, reminders/no-show; Fathom transcripts → `event.body_text`.
9. **WhatsApp/SMS capture** — the dominant Indian B2B channel into the spine.
10. **Identity-resolution engine** — resolve same person/company across call/email/LinkedIn/meeting; the keystone of "ONE place."
11. **Segmentation** — dynamic/static lists, AND/OR/nested filters, suppression, list-as-trigger.
12. **Reporting/dashboard builder** — no-code reports, pivots, cross-object, scheduled, shared metrics/semantic layer (so analytics + AI agree).
13. **Import wizard + fuzzy dedup + merge UI**; integration OAuth/secrets/token-refresh framework; calendar/LinkedIn/Slack connectors.
14. **Compliance** — DPDP/GDPR recording-consent, retention/purge/RTBF for transcripts, field-level PII encryption-at-rest, data-residency, AI-access audit.

## PHASE 4 — INTELLIGENCE (the native differentiator — pure add-on over the spine)
*Goal: derive intelligence + intent from captured conversations. Reads `event.body_text` only; writes results back as events.*

1. **pgvector + embeddings pipeline** — `event_embedding`; AI worker embeds new events from the outbox; semantic search/RAG replaces keyword search.
2. **Explainability/provenance primitive** — every AI output cites its source interaction(s); interaction-provenance tagging (human/extension/telephony/AI).
3. **Transcription/diarization pipeline** — owned recording storage → transcript → `body_text` (calls + meetings).
4. **First-party intent scoring** — reply sentiment, meeting cadence, buying-signal/objection/competitor phrase extraction → `intent_signal` events.
5. **Scoring framework** — separate explainable fit / engagement / intent scores as `field_value` (surfaced in lists/Kanban for free).
6. **Conversation intelligence** — call/meeting summarization, objection/competitor taxonomy, deal-risk/stalled/sentiment-trend detection.
7. **Buying-committee mapping** — champion/blocker/economic-buyer from conversations.
8. **Next-best-action + manager digests** — recommendations + momentum/health signals; similar-accounts engine.
9. **Model-quality loop** — drift/precision monitoring, human-correction capture for retraining.
10. **MCP + agent interface** — thin wrapper over `/api/v1` + event stream; agent-readable semantic layer/data dictionary; platform-level write-safety/dry-run/approval + action-audit + reversibility guardrails.

---
